message:
refactor(web): UI components now derive shortcuts from ActionRegistry

Refactors key UI components to use actionSurfaces module for shortcut
display, eliminating hardcoded shortcut strings:

AnalysisSidebar.tsx:
- Now uses ANALYSIS_SIDEBAR_CATEGORIES from actionSurfaces
- Renders shortcut hints via formatActionShortcutForSurface()
- Category-based grouping with experience-level gating
- Removed ~100 lines of hardcoded tool definitions
- Each tool links to its ActionId for single-source-of-truth shortcuts

Footer.tsx:
- Now uses APP_SHELL_FOOTER_HINTS from actionSurfaces
- Renders footer hints via formatHintKeys()
- Platform-aware display (⌘ on macOS, Ctrl on others)
- Removed hardcoded shortcut strings like "j/k", "?", "/"
- Dynamic hint generation from ActionRegistry

ActionDrawer.tsx (mobile):
- Now uses ACTION_DRAWER_SECTIONS from actionSurfaces
- Section-based organization (View, Sequence, Analysis, Tools)
- Respects closeAfter and labelStrategy per-action config
- Shortcut hints via formatActionShortcutForSurface()
- Removed duplicate action definitions

BlockedHotkeyToast.tsx:
- Simplified to use ActionRegistry for action lookup
- Shows action title from registry when hotkey is blocked
- Cleaner implementation without inline shortcut formatting

BottomSheet.tsx:
- Improved gesture handling for swipe-to-close
- Better integration with overlay stack system
- Consistent close behavior with desktop overlays

Benefits:
- Zero shortcut duplication across components
- Platform-aware shortcut display everywhere
- Single source of truth in ActionRegistry
- Easier to add/modify shortcuts in one place
- Reduced component complexity and bundle size

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
